verificationNote: Financial services cloud security requirements constructed from BNM RMiT (2020, updated 2023), PDPA 2010, NACSA Act 854, and industry best practices. These are indicative interpretations -- verify against current BNM directives and sector lead guidance before use in compliance assessments.
nacsaSectorLead: Bank Negara Malaysia (BNM) / Securities Commission Malaysia (SC)
applicableStandards: CSA Cloud Controls Matrix v4 (CCM v4)
PCI DSS v4.0 (payment card data -- applies regardless of hosting model)

rmitSections.title: Cloud Services
rmitSections.description: Dedicated section in RMiT addressing cloud adoption by financial institutions. Covers risk assessment, due diligence, data residency, exit strategy, and ongoing monitoring of cloud service providers.
rmitSections.cloudImplication: All material cloud outsourcing must comply with RMiT-10 requirements. Financial institutions must notify BNM before adopting cloud for material operations. Cloud risk assessment must be conducted before adoption and reviewed annually.

rmitSections.title: Data Residency
rmitSections.description: Critical customer data and its backups must reside in Malaysia. Metadata and derived analytics must also be considered for data residency requirements.
rmitSections.cloudImplication: CSP must provide guaranteed Malaysian data residency for critical customer data. Organisations must verify that CSP services do not replicate data outside Malaysia without explicit configuration. This affects CSP region selection and service configuration.

rmitSections.title: Vendor Risk Assessment
rmitSections.description: Financial institutions must conduct comprehensive risk assessment of CSPs covering security controls, business continuity, data protection, and regulatory compliance.
rmitSections.cloudImplication: CSP due diligence must include: SOC 2 Type II review, security architecture assessment, data handling practices, subprocessor management, and incident response capabilities. Annual reassessment required.

rmitSections.title: Exit Strategy
rmitSections.description: Financial institutions must maintain a viable exit strategy for material cloud services including data portability, alternative providers, and transition timeline.
rmitSections.cloudImplication: Exit strategy must be documented, tested, and reviewed annually. Must include: data export procedures, application portability assessment, cost estimate for migration, and transition timeline. CSP lock-in must be actively managed.

rmitSections.title: Incident Reporting
rmitSections.description: Technology-related incidents affecting financial services must be reported to BNM within 24 hours for major incidents.
rmitSections.cloudImplication: Cloud security incidents affecting financial operations must be reported to BNM within 24 hours (major incidents). For NCII-designated financial infrastructure, NACSA s26 requires 6-hour notification. Dual reporting obligations must be coordinated.

rmitSections.title: Encryption Standards
rmitSections.description: Customer data must be encrypted at rest and in transit using industry-standard encryption. Key management must remain under the financial institution's control.
rmitSections.cloudImplication: All customer data in cloud must use AES-256 encryption at rest and TLS 1.2+ in transit. Customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) required for critical data -- CSP-managed keys alone are insufficient. Key management procedures must be documented.

rmitSections.title: Cybersecurity Operations
rmitSections.description: Covers cybersecurity monitoring, threat detection, incident response, and vulnerability management for financial institutions.
rmitSections.cloudImplication: Cloud security monitoring must be integrated with the institution's SOC. Cloud audit logs must feed into SIEM. Cloud-specific threat detection (GuardDuty, Defender, SCC) must be deployed. 24/7 monitoring coverage for cloud infrastructure.

rmitSections.title: Technology Audit
rmitSections.description: Internal and external technology audits must cover cloud infrastructure and services.
rmitSections.cloudImplication: Cloud security must be included in annual technology audit scope. Auditors must have capability to assess cloud security posture. CSP SOC 2 reports support but do not replace the institution's own audit obligations.

keyRequirements.name: Data residency in Malaysia
keyRequirements.description: BNM RMiT requires critical customer data and its backups to reside in Malaysia. This is a non-negotiable requirement for financial institutions using cloud services.
implementation: Configure all cloud services to use Malaysian region (ap-southeast-3 for AWS if available, Southeast Asia for Azure, asia-southeast1 for GCP)
If CSP does not have Malaysian region, nearest approved region (Singapore) may be used with BNM notification -- subject to BNM approval
Verify that metadata, logs, and derived analytics also comply with data residency requirements
Implement region restriction policies (SCPs, Azure Policy) to prevent data creation in non-approved regions
Monitor for CSP service updates that may change data replication behaviour

keyRequirements.name: Vendor risk assessment for CSP
keyRequirements.description: Comprehensive risk assessment of the CSP must be conducted before adoption and reviewed annually. Must cover security, operational resilience, financial stability, and regulatory compliance.
implementation: Request and review CSP SOC 2 Type II report (under NDA)
Review CSP ISO 27001, ISO 27017, and CSA STAR certifications
Assess CSP incident response capabilities and notification timelines
Evaluate CSP financial stability and business continuity capabilities
Review CSP subprocessor management and fourth-party risk
Document vendor risk assessment with findings and acceptance/mitigation decisions

keyRequirements.name: Cloud exit strategy
keyRequirements.description: Maintain a viable, tested exit strategy for all material cloud services. Must include data portability, alternative providers, timeline, and cost estimate.
implementation: Document data export procedures for each material cloud service
Identify alternative CSPs or on-premises options for each service
Estimate migration cost and timeline for exit scenario
Test data export procedures annually to verify functionality
Design for portability: use open standards (Kubernetes, SQL, REST APIs) over proprietary CSP services where practical
Include exit strategy review in annual cloud governance review

keyRequirements.name: Incident reporting (BNM 24h + NACSA 6h)
keyRequirements.description: Cloud security incidents affecting financial operations must be reported to BNM within 24 hours for major incidents. NCII-designated financial infrastructure requires NACSA s26 notification within 6 hours.
implementation: Establish dual notification procedure for both BNM and NACSA (if NCII-designated)
NACSA 6-hour deadline takes priority -- notify NACSA first, then BNM
Define cloud incident classification matrix aligned with both BNM and NACSA thresholds
Pre-configure incident notification templates for both regulators
Test notification procedures quarterly through tabletop exercises
Establish CSP incident notification relay to ensure CSP-detected incidents reach organisation quickly

keyRequirements.name: Encryption and key management
keyRequirements.description: All customer data must be encrypted at rest (AES-256) and in transit (TLS 1.2+). Financial institution must maintain control over encryption keys for critical data.
implementation: Enable encryption at rest for all cloud storage using AES-256
Use customer-managed encryption keys (CMEK) for critical customer data -- not CSP-managed default keys
Implement TLS 1.2+ for all data in transit (disable TLS 1.0 and 1.1)
Document key management procedures including rotation, access control, and backup
Consider client-side encryption for highest-sensitivity data (data encrypted before upload to CSP)
Implement key usage logging and monitoring

nacsaObligations.description: NACSA obligations for NCII-designated financial infrastructure using cloud services.
nacsaObligations.applicability: Applies only to financial infrastructure designated as NCII under NACSA Act 854. Not all financial institutions are NCII-designated -- designation is determined by NACSA and the sector lead (BNM).
obligations: s17: Accept NCII designation and comply with all Act 854 obligations
s18: Implement appropriate cybersecurity measures for cloud-hosted NCII -- cloud does not reduce the obligation
s21: Include cloud-hosted NCII assets in annual risk assessment
s23: Include cloud security in biennial NACSA audit scope
s24: Share cloud threat intelligence with NACSA when directed
s26: Notify NACSA within 6 hours of significant cloud security incident affecting NCII

sectorSpecificThreats.threat: Payment system compromise through cloud infrastructure
Customer data exfiltration from cloud-hosted core banking
Regulatory non-compliance through cloud data residency violation
API abuse in open banking cloud integration
